tipRules.tip: Yamamoto pro Shryock: 'The Yamatanuki is 3.5 inches at 5/8 oz — heavy enough to fish weightless on casting gear. It's a cross between a Senko and a jig with the profile of a small bluegill'
Yamamoto pro Shryock: 'The Yamatanuki excels at skipping to suspended fish under docks and marinas — it's heavy enough to reach fish suspended deep in gnarly marina slips'
Yamamoto pro Shryock: 'Use a 7-foot 3-inch MH casting rod, 19lb fluorocarbon leader tied to 40lb braid, EWG hook. Emphasize hookset more than with a jig'
Yamamoto pro Shryock: 'The 2.5-inch Yamatanuki on a ball head or wobble head falls and spirals like a tube — big deal for smallmouth'

knowledge.insight: Yamamoto pro Shryock: The Yamatanuki is a heavy 'gravity bait' — 3.5 inches at 5/8 oz with a signature side-to-side flutter on the fall. Works from 1 foot to 20+ feet suspended. Extremely weedless. Can replace jigs around heavy cover and grass

Yamamoto pro Shryock: 'It's a power-finesse approach — the power part comes in your tackle. You don't have to downsize. You can throw it on a spinning rod but it's almost borderline heavy for a soft spinning rod. I like using a baitcaster'
Yamamoto pro Shryock: Unlike a Senko, the Yamatanuki has a fast fall rate so it excels in nearly any depth. It's non-disruptive like a Senko in shallow water but can also target deep suspended fish
